title: Search next/prev buttons reset _matchIdx on every click
description:
Click handlers for the search-next/search-prev toolbar buttons (and the Enter key in the search input) call this.search(value).next() / .prev(). Because search() invokes _runSearch internally, which rebuilds matches and jumps to index 0, clicking ↓ twice always lands on match 1 — match 2+ are unreachable via the UI.

The debounced input handler does this correctly (calls _runSearch directly). Fix: the button/keyboard handlers should call _gotoMatch((_matchIdx ± 1 + count) % count, query, options) against the existing _matches array, not re-invoke search().

Files: src/json-viewer.js around lines 904-905 (button handlers) and the keydown handler in _bindEvents.

Found in final code review of json-viewer v0.1 migration. Pre-existing behavior from the seed; not introduced by this migration. UX bug — search button navigation is currently broken past the first match.

title: Extract test/fixtures/ and expand Playwright coverage
description:
The spec (docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-12-json-viewer-design.md) lists 10 Playwright test sections and a test/fixtures/ directory containing api.json, config.json, nested.json, array.json, exotic.json (BigInt/Date/Map/Set/RegExp/Function/circular), and huge.json (2000 keys). The v0.1 migration shipped only 4 of those sections (mount, Map/Set fidelity, search hits, search-doesn't-expandAll) using inline data in test-page.html.

Work to do:
1. Extract fixtures from docs/index.html's embedded samples into test/fixtures/.
2. Reroute existing specs to load fixtures via fetch (relative URL).
3. Add new spec sections covering: lazy expansion (only configured depth eager), full path-API matrix (string/glob/RegExp/fn matchers), keyclick + valueclick events (composed: true, correct detail shapes), copy buttons (path / value / 'path = value' snippet, BigInt-safe), token contract (overrides resolve through the --json-viewer-* > --vb-* > hardcoded chain), mobile container query (toolbar labels hide at 360px, tap targets ≥44px), attribute reactivity (mode/expanded/show-types runtime changes).

Found in final code review of json-viewer v0.1 migration. Explicitly deferred in the plan's self-review section.

title: Convert lazy <details> toggle listener to { once: true }
description:
In src/json-viewer.js around line 698, the deferred-render handler on lazy <details> elements is registered with { once: false }. The _renderedSet WeakSet correctly prevents double-rendering, but the listener itself accumulates on every lazy node and is never removed.

For a large tree this means N persistent listeners on N <details> elements. Fix: change { once: false } to { once: true } — the listener cleans itself up after first fire, and _renderedSet remains as a safety guard.

Found in final code review of json-viewer v0.1 migration (see HANDOFF.md status update 2026-05-14). Pre-existing behavior from the seed; not a regression from the migration.
